title: Turning Old Car Aluminum into New Car Parts
summary: Scientists have found a way to reuse old aluminum from cars to make new car parts. When cars get old, their aluminum body panels are often thrown away because they are too dirty to use again. This is a problem because aluminum is important for making things like energy systems and new vehicles. Researchers at Oak Ridge National Laboratory created a new material called RidgeAlloy. They melt down the old aluminum and mix it to make it strong and safe for car parts. This helps save energy and reduces waste. By 2030, many cars with aluminum will be old, so this new method can turn scrap into useful material for making new cars in the United States.

questions.question: Why is old aluminum from cars often not reused?
What does recycling aluminum help save?
By what year are many aluminum cars expected to be old?
What is aluminum used for in cars?

background_read: Aluminum is a metal that is light and strong, often used in cars to make them lighter and save fuel. When cars are old, they are taken apart, and the aluminum can be recycled. Recycling means using old materials to make new things, which helps the environment by reducing waste and saving energy. Scientists work in labs to find better ways to recycle materials like aluminum so we can use them again in important ways, like building new cars.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists at Oak Ridge National Laboratory
WHAT: Created RidgeAlloy to recycle old car aluminum into new car parts
WHY: To save energy, reduce waste, and make new cars from scrap aluminum

perspectives.description: Recycling aluminum saves energy and reduces waste, helping the planet.
